title: calophyllum tournanense
intro: Calophyllum tournanense, also known as the Tournan's calophyllum, is a species of evergreen tree native to the island of Madagascar. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seed oil, which is rich in calophyllic acid and other bioactive compounds. The seed oil of Calophyllum tournanense has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin and wound infections, as well as fever and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can provide health benefits such as improved wound healing, reduced fever, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of Calophyllum tournanense, including essential oil and extracts, are used in traditional medicine and can be found in various forms, including capsules, tinctures, and topical creams.
